module_a.name: consensus_voting
module_a.verdict: POSITIVE in-sample signal, BH-significant — not yet confirmed in OOS

module_b.name: slot_effectiveness
module_b.verdict: Slot 3 and 4 significantly lower hit rate than slot 1 (descriptive, not OOS-validated)

module_c.name: recent_window
module_c.verdict: No significant difference between recent and full-history ensemble performance

module_e.name: main_special_separated
module_e.verdict: Main-number signal p_bh=0.024 (significant); Special-number no signal

module_f.name: walk_forward_oos
module_f.reason_failed: OOS Window 2 has only 499 draws (1499 total − 1000 training = 499 < 500 required minimum). Stability check across ≥ 2 windows cannot be performed. The failure is due to data volume limitation, not absence of positive direction.

decision.null_reason: Module F final gate failed: OOS Window 2 has 499 draws, 1 draw below the pre-declared 500-draw minimum. Only 1 of the required ≥ 2 OOS windows was computed. Stability cannot be confirmed. Per the pre-declared P166 protocol, this is a NULL result.
decision.promising_signal_note: The ensemble voting approach shows a consistent positive direction (in-sample p_bh=0.038, OOS Window 1 p_bh=0.049, both above random and best single strategy). This is encouraging but does not qualify as a confirmed defensible edge under the strict P166 OOS protocol.
decision.interpretation_caveat: The OOS Window 2 shortfall (499 vs 500 draws) is 1 draw. This is a tight margin. It does NOT mean the result is borderline valid — the pre-declared threshold was 500 and it was not met. Any adjustment to the threshold must be pre-registered in a new plan (P169) before re-evaluation, not applied retroactively.

options_evaluation.label: WAIT_FOR_MORE_DRAWS
options_evaluation.description: Wait for ≥ 1 more POWER_LOTTO draw to be added to the dataset (≥ 1500 complete draws total), then re-run Module F with OOS Window 2 covering ≥ 500 draws.
pros: Strictly respects the pre-declared P166 OOS protocol without any retroactive modification
Adds real prospective data — not post-hoc rationalization
If OOS Window 2 also passes, the result becomes genuinely stable across 2 windows
Lowest risk of governance integrity violation
cons: Research is blocked until new draw data is available
POWER_LOTTO draws may be infrequent — wait time is uncertain
The positive signal from Window 1 may not persist in genuinely new data
options_evaluation.risk: SHORT_TERM_RESEARCH_STALL — acceptable if signal is real
options_evaluation.when_to_choose: If the user wants the strictest possible protocol adherence and is willing to wait for new draws
options_evaluation.required_authorization: No explicit phrase required to wait — this is the default passive state

options_evaluation.label: PLAN_ONLY_THRESHOLD_SENSITIVITY_REVIEW
options_evaluation.description: Produce a plan-only (P169) document that evaluates threshold sensitivity: what would the result be if OOS window minimum was 400, 450, or 500 draws? Report the sensitivity range descriptively without changing the P167 verdict.
pros: Determines whether the 499 vs 500 draw boundary is material (i.e., does it change the conclusion or is the effect stable across nearby thresholds?)
Does not retroactively change P167 outcome — P167 NULL result stands
Informs future protocol design without gaming the current result
cons: Easily misread as retroactive justification for claiming P167 passed — must be framed carefully
Sensitivity analysis that consistently shows 'would have passed at 499' could create pressure to lower thresholds
Requires careful governance language to prevent threshold shopping
options_evaluation.risk: MEDIUM — threshold-shopping risk if not carefully framed as descriptive plan only
options_evaluation.when_to_choose: If the user wants to understand the robustness of the 500-draw threshold before deciding on Option A or D
options_evaluation.required_authorization: YES produce P169 threshold sensitivity plan only, no rerun of P167 verdict

options_evaluation.label: NARROW_TO_CONSENSUS_MAIN_SIGNAL_TRACKING
options_evaluation.description: Narrow the research focus to the two strongest signals from P167 (Module A consensus voting and Module E main-number signal). Pre-register a prospective tracking protocol: for each new POWER_LOTTO draw, record whether the consensus ensemble hit count is above the P161 mean. Evaluate after ≥ 100 prospective draws.
pros: Focuses on the most promising directions rather than starting from scratch
Prospective tracking prevents in-sample selection bias
Pre-registration ensures the tracking is not post-hoc
100-draw prospective target is achievable on a reasonable timeline
cons: Requires pre-registering the tracking protocol before any new data is examined
100 draws may not be sufficient for statistical power (power analysis needed)
Risk of over-indexing on two signals that may have been noise
options_evaluation.risk: MEDIUM — cherry-picking risk if tracking protocol is not strictly pre-registered
options_evaluation.when_to_choose: If the user wants to continue research in a focused, prospective way without waiting for large data batches
options_evaluation.required_authorization: YES plan P169 prospective signal tracking for consensus voting and main-number, no rerun

options_evaluation.label: PAUSE_R1_POWER_LOTTO_RESEARCH
options_evaluation.description: Formally pause the R1 POWER_LOTTO research track. Archive current findings (P161-P168) as INCONCLUSIVE. Resume only if: (1) significant new data becomes available (≥ 500 new draws), or (2) a qualitatively different hypothesis emerges.
pros: Prevents over-mining a dataset that has shown borderline-but-unconfirmed signals
Frees resources for other lottery types or research directions
Honest acknowledgment that current signal is insufficient for deployment
cons: Halts a research track that shows a genuine positive direction
The positive signal from Module A and E may be real but require more data — pausing risks missing it
Once paused, re-entry requires new authorization and context rebuild
options_evaluation.risk: LOW research waste risk; MEDIUM opportunity cost if signal is real
options_evaluation.when_to_choose: If the user judges that the cost of continued research exceeds the expected value of confirming the weak signal

reasoning: The 499 vs 500 threshold gap is 1 draw — a sensitivity review (Option B) will determine whether this boundary is material or if the result is stable across nearby thresholds. This should be done as a descriptive plan ONLY, with P167 NULL verdict preserved.

Simultaneously, the Module A and Module E signals are the strongest findings. Pre-registering a prospective tracking protocol (Option C) allows future data to be evaluated cleanly without in-sample selection.
Option A (wait for draws) is implicit in both B and C — if new draws arrive during P169 planning, they can be incorporated.
Option D (pause) is premature given the positive direction. Pausing before attempting the less-costly sensitivity review and prospective tracking would leave a promising signal unexplored.
p169_scope_constraints: P169 must be plan-only — no re-running P167 analysis with different thresholds and claiming a different classification
P169 must NOT change the P167 final classification to anything other than P167_POWER_LOTTO_NO_DEFENSIBLE_EDGE_FOUND
P169 threshold sensitivity results must be clearly labeled RETROSPECTIVE DESCRIPTIVE — not as retroactive gate passage
P169 prospective tracking plan must specify: metric, threshold, N, correction method, and what constitutes success BEFORE any new data is examined

next_task_note: P168 defines the decision review. P169 may not begin without explicit user authorization. P169 must be plan-only — no re-running analysis, no changing P167 verdict, no deploying strategies.
